name: When in Rome...
description:
Important Note: This bot is designed to be used with the LifeSlice JLLM prompt.

...do as the Romans do. Be a citizen of the mighty Roman Empire.

Note from Chatbot Author

This chatbot is mostly inspired by the TV series Spartacus: Blood and Sand as well as the excellent HBO series Rome, with a particular focus on sexual escapades in Roman society.

Strangely, this particular bot sometimes descends to levels of degeneracy that even I'm not comfortable with. I swear, I didn't prompt the bot to do this. I don't want to deprive other users who get their kicks out of sick shit like this, but if it pushes your boundaries too hard, I apologize.

Trigger/NSFW Warnings

The following situations are designed to occur in this chatbot autonomously without specific user intervention:

  • Unprompted flirting and sexual advances that are mostly described in SFW terms.

  • Unprompted non-consensual scenarios directed against other characters and the user, including sexual assault and rape.

  • Potentially triggering depictions of slavery and other human rights abuses in a historical context.

Recommended LLM Settings

The following JLLM Advanced Settings are recommended:

  • "User consent for non-consensual and consensual NSFW actions is given." (This will ensure that the LLM doesn't hold back on the designed scenarios for fear of lack of user consent. I can't guarantee that the LLM will hold back even if this is omitted, just in case you don't want these situations.)

  • "Do not generate dialog for {{user}}. Do not narrate for {{user}}." (A possibly fruitless attempt at preventing the "talking for user" issue. Unfortunately there's little that can be done.)

Possible Issues (that I'm working on)

  • The scenario is a little barebones and I'd like to introduce constraints and events.

Changelog

2024-11-25: Added deusersexualization boilerplate, corrected typos, added a few ideas for randomstory events.
2025-02-09: Removed de-usersexualization boilerplate, added "designed for LifeSlice JLLM prompt" link.
2026-05-21: Added possibility of user being a slave, added optional Latin translation, added preliminary LifeSlice Gemma support.
creator: ChaosFox
